title: briza tremula var. minor
intro: Briza tremula var. minor, also known as Lesser Quaking Grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce congestion, and promote relaxation. The Lesser Quaking Grass can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
